# UC-22.2.37 · NIS2 SCADA System Access Monitoring

## Description

Interactive access to SCADA/HMI layers is high risk. This use case monitors engineering workstation logons, HMI tag writes, and remote-desktop sessions to SCADA hosts — correlating with maintenance windows and approved technician identities.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes win_sec and sourcetypes cyberark:session. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Inventory SCADA hosts with roles in `nis2_scada_asset_inventory.csv`; (2) ingest maintenance windows from plant maintenance system; (3) alert on interactive access outside windows; (4) integrate PAM session video references for investigations; (5) align with safety permit-to-work where applicable. Indexes required: `index=win_sec`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cyberark:session`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (Splunkbase 2891).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4624/4648, `cyberark:session`, `nis2_scada_maintenance_windows.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Inventory SCADA hosts with roles in `nis2_scada_asset_inventory.csv`; (2) ingest maintenance windows from plant maintenance system; (3) alert on interactive access outside windows; (4) integrate PAM session video references for investigations; (5) align with safety permit-to-work where applicable.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=win_sec EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=10 OR Logon_Type=2) OR sourcetype="cyberark:session" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(dest_nt_host, ComputerName, target_host)
| lookup nis2_scada_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT scada_role plant_site
| where scada_role IN ("HMI","ENG_WORKSTATION","SCADA_SERVER")
| join host [
    | inputlookup nis2_scada_maintenance_windows.csv
    | eval starttime=strptime(start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval endtime=strptime(end,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
]
| where _time < starttime OR _time > endtime
| stats values(user) as users count by host plant_site
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 SCADA System Access Monitoring (Art. 21(2)(a))** — Interactive access to SCADA/HMI layers is high risk. This use case monitors engineering workstation logons, HMI tag writes, and remote-desktop sessions to SCADA hosts — correlating with maintenance windows and approved technician identities.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4624/4648, `cyberark:session`, `nis2_scada_maintenance_windows.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (Splunkbase 2891). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: win_sec; **sourcetype**: cyberark:session. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=win_sec, sourcetype="cyberark:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **host** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where scada_role IN ("HMI","ENG_WORKSTATION","SCADA_SERVER")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where _time < starttime OR _time > endtime` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **host plant_site**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: endtime, host, plant_site, starttime, users.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (out-of-window access), Timeline (sessions), Single value (distinct technicians), Map (plant sites).

## SPL

```spl
(index=win_sec EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=10 OR Logon_Type=2) OR sourcetype="cyberark:session" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(dest_nt_host, ComputerName, target_host)
| lookup nis2_scada_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT scada_role plant_site
| where scada_role IN ("HMI","ENG_WORKSTATION","SCADA_SERVER")
| join host [
    | inputlookup nis2_scada_maintenance_windows.csv
    | eval starttime=strptime(start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval endtime=strptime(end,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
]
| where _time < starttime OR _time > endtime
| stats values(user) as users count by host plant_site
| sort - count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (out-of-window access), Timeline (sessions), Single value (distinct technicians), Map (plant sites). Key fields: `endtime`, `host`, `plant_site`, `starttime`, `users`.
